risk_factors: No prior systemic anti-cancer therapy for advanced disease
Active or prior documented autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within 2 years
Systemic corticosteroid therapy > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent within 14 days of first dose
Active hepatitis B or C infection
simplification_suggestions: Consider broadening age range to 18-80 years, given improving healthspan and life expectancy
Allow prior systemic anti-cancer therapy for advanced disease, considering disease-free interval and response
Include patients with treated, stable brain metastases, ensuring close monitoring for CNS progression
Extend timeframe for autoimmune disease treatment or consider asymptomatic, well-controlled conditions
Relax corticosteroid dosage threshold to 20 mg/day prednisone equivalent, balancing immunosuppression and trial participation
Consider enrolling patients with well-controlled hepatitis B or C, using antiviral therapy and regular monitoring
Expand prior malignancy exclusion to 5 years, allowing more patients to participate while minimizing confounding
estimated_population_impact: eliminates ~10% of target patients at older age
narrows by ~20% due to prior therapy restrictions
eliminates ~20% of target patients with CNS involvement
excludes ~10% of target patients with autoimmune conditions
restricts ~5% of target patients on high-dose corticosteroids
excludes ~5% of target patients with active hepatitis
eliminates ~15% of target patients with prior malignancies
